description: This stacked horizontal bar chart quantifies the relative surface oxide composition, derived from XPS peak-area calculations, for Fe–30Mn–8Al–1C low-density steels containing 0 wt% Cu (0Cu), 1 wt% Cu (1Cu), and 3 wt% Cu (3Cu). Each bar is color-coded by phase—Fe₃O₄, Fe₂O₃, FeOOH, MnO, MnO₂, Al₂O₃, Cu₂O, and CuO—with percentages explicitly labeled. In 0Cu, Al₂O₃ is highest at 38.2%, MnO and MnO₂ together constitute 47%, and total Fe oxides are ~14.7%. In 1Cu, Mn oxides increase to ~57.2% (MnO 27.7%, MnO₂ 29.5%), Al₂O₃ decreases to 28.2%, and Cu₂O and CuO emerge at 1.2% and 1%, respectively. In 3Cu, Mn oxides dominate at ~77% (MnO 38%, MnO₂ 39%), Al₂O₃ drops sharply to 16.3%, Fe oxides fall to ~5.2%, and Cu oxides total ~1.5%. The data demonstrate that Cu addition progressively raises Mn oxide formation while diminishing protective Al₂O₃ and Fe oxide contents, consistent with the reported increase in corrosion sites from carbide refinement and grain-boundary Cu enrichment that accelerates localized corrosion.
